acceptance_targets: [ch17-F01] PASS if the revised lines corresponding to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:221-255 contain at least one irreversible on-page state change before reconvening (not just verbal pressure), and no repeated restatement of the same stability argument in two consecutive speeches; FAIL otherwise.

[ch17-F02] PASS if revised versions of these spans show at least two live consequence events on-page: one legal/physical restriction and one economic/professional loss, each with actor-action-object specificity; FAIL if consequences remain primarily notification summaries.

[ch17-F03] PASS if the revised section includes at least one additional concrete injury or near-injury beat plus one explicit tactical action that causes the retreat before the equivalent of line 199; FAIL if de-escalation still arrives as summary-only narration.

[ch17-F04] PASS if no more than two consecutive one-word Q/A pairs remain in the revised cross-exam opening and at least one exchange forces Im to pivot off his prepared sequence immediately after; FAIL otherwise.

[ch17-F05] PASS if a blind read of at least five revised lines drawn from those spans allows correct speaker attribution for at least four lines based on syntax/vocabulary alone (without dialogue tags); FAIL otherwise.

[ch17-F06] PASS if the revised text at the cited spans contains at most two 'like ...' similes total and each remaining simile contributes unique sensory information not duplicated in adjacent literal description; FAIL otherwise.

[ch17-F07] PASS if each targeted span includes at least two explicit Ina interiority beats and each beat directly changes her next spoken line or physical decision in the following paragraph; FAIL if either span remains predominantly external/reportorial without action-linked interior response.
[FBR-007] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7118 conveys institutional tension without "went still/flat" phrasing. Fail if the default freeze construction persists.
[FBR-009] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7162-7165 removes "landed like" while retaining pressure escalation. Fail if the sentence still uses the same formula template.

[FBR-013] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7162-7168 feels like a fresh argumentative turn tied to the hearing evidence rather than recycled chapter-05/09 rhetoric. Fail if the exchange still reads as repetition of earlier tokenized framing.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised lines corresponding to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:221-255 contain at least one irreversible on-page state change before reconvening (not just verbal pressure), and no repeated restatement of the same stability argument in two consecutive speeches; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: The Chamber B detour reiterates arguments already made in the hearing (stability vs disclosure) without producing a new irreversible turn, so momentum stalls before the chapter's decisive ruling sequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:221 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:255, either compress the negotiation to a brief beat or force a concrete new state change (for example: immediate condition served in writing, compelled surrender action, or a legal waiver demand that changes afternoon stakes). Keep one rhetorical frame only; remove duplicate 'stability' paraphrases already covered in the hearing.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised versions of these spans show at least two live consequence events on-page: one legal/physical restriction and one economic/professional loss, each with actor-action-object specificity; FAIL if consequences remain primarily notification summaries.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift in the fallout: major costs (indictment constraints, blacklist cascade, trial exposure) are frequently delivered as clean administrative summaries and phone notices rather than fully embodied scene consequences.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:379 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:386, and at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:471 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:475, replace summary delivery with enacted consequence beats (for example: explicit service of release conditions, physical handover/check procedure, immediate financial call or access failure with concrete stakes). Keep legal facts, but stage their bodily and practical impact in real time.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised section includes at least one additional concrete injury or near-injury beat plus one explicit tactical action that causes the retreat before the equivalent of line 199; FAIL if de-escalation still arrives as summary-only narration.
findings.problem: Timidity after escalation: the plaza sequence builds to imminent riot and bodily harm, then resolves in quick summary language that reduces the scene's immediate physical stakes.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:185 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:199, add one more concrete, stepwise beat of danger and consequence from Ina's close POV before containment, and make the de-escalation visibly causal (specific tactical move, not abstract crowd summary).

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two consecutive one-word Q/A pairs remain in the revised cross-exam opening and at least one exchange forces Im to pivot off his prepared sequence immediately after; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: Opening cross-examination leans into transcript cadence (stacked yes/no turns), delaying leverage change and flattening dramatic pressure despite high stakes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:23 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:53 so interrogation includes interruption, evasion pressure, and at least one tactical counter-move by Ina before the chain-of-custody explanation. Preserve legal realism but reduce uninterrupted yes/no volleying.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a blind read of at least five revised lines drawn from those spans allows correct speaker attribution for at least four lines based on syntax/vocabulary alone (without dialogue tags); FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: Im, Kwon, and Nam converge on similar high-formal abstraction language, blurring idiolect separation across institutional voices in a chapter that depends on rhetorical contrast.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:133 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:134, and at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:223 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:247, sharpen speaker-specific diction: Kwon should carry historical inevitability cadence; Im should sound prosecutorial and evidentiary; Nam should sound bureaucratically mitigative and euphemistic.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised text at the cited spans contains at most two 'like ...' similes total and each remaining simile contributes unique sensory information not duplicated in adjacent literal description; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: Recurring simile template ('like ...') appears far beyond the allowed repetition threshold, creating a predictable sentence engine and flattening paragraph texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise imagery at the cited lines to diversify construction types (verb-led concrete description, metonymic detail, sensory specificity without simile). Keep no more than two 'like ...' similes across these targeted beats.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each targeted span includes at least two explicit Ina interiority beats and each beat directly changes her next spoken line or physical decision in the following paragraph; FAIL if either span remains predominantly external/reportorial without action-linked interior response.
findings.problem: The chapter contains extended externalized blocks that run past the 10-paragraph interiority threshold, making portions read like procedural transcript instead of close-third lived experience.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:23 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:74, and in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:259 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:333, insert brief Ina-centered interior beats (thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment) before or after major testimony pivots so external events clearly alter her internal state and next action.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7118 conveys institutional tension without "went still/flat" phrasing. Fail if the default freeze construction persists.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book pattern (5 chapters: 07, 09, 10, 13, 17): default facial-freeze construction. This chapter's instance should be REPLACED.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7118 so the liaison's reaction is rendered with non-template procedural behavior (note-taking change, interruption choice, mic posture) rather than "went still."

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7162-7165 removes "landed like" while retaining pressure escalation. Fail if the sentence still uses the same formula template.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book pattern (3 chapters: 02, 17, 18): formula metaphor template "landed like X". This chapter's instance should be REPLACED.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7162-7165 to keep the coldness of Kwon's intervention without the "landed like" formula; prefer courtroom-specific physical perception (temperature, hearing, posture shift) over stock metaphor shape.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7162-7168 feels like a fresh argumentative turn tied to the hearing evidence rather than recycled chapter-05/09 rhetoric. Fail if the exchange still reads as repetition of earlier tokenized framing.
findings.problem: Style-token overfitting pattern (recurs across chapters 05, 09, 17): the same discipline/stability abstraction set reappears in climactic testimony, blunting rhetorical escalation at the exact point that should feel newly dangerous.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7162-7168, vary Kwon-vs-Ina language away from repeated abstraction tokens and anchor the conflict in hearing-room specifics (evidence handling, chain-of-custody claims, human cost) to avoid mantra carryover.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
